style.detail: em dash in test comment (line 2765); rewritten to two clauses, re-run PASS, committed c9b2fcf

surfaces.detail: browser.rs is not a feature surface in the map (flags/main/commands only)

siblings:discarded.detail: hits are vendored CDP protocol JSONs (browser_protocol.json, js_protocol.json) defining the wire `discarded` field, plus #1532 pre-existing browser.rs code the fix builds on. No behavior text to sync.

callers:active_page_index.detail: contract change = active_page_index may be non-zero after connect (was always 0). All 20 consumers use it as a bounds-checked index into self.pages; none assume post-connect==0. find_live_page_index always returns an in-range index.

findings.summary: Em dash in added test comment violates the no-dash house norm.
findings.evidence: gate.sh style flagged line 2765; norm: prefer rewriting to avoid dashes.
findings.resolution: Fixed in c9b2fcf (rewritten to two clauses). Style gate re-run PASS.

verification.tests_run: cargo test test_connect (20 tests) all pass; 5 new #1036 regression tests green
verification.drift_forced_red: Reverted the fix (active=0 blindly); test_connect_skips_discarded_first_target times out (Elapsed) at 15s. Guard is real. Restored.

dogfood_built_artifact.discard_repro: chrome://discards is gated + shadow-DOM; instead killed real renderers with CDP Page.crash on tracked https tabs (keeps https URL so should_track_target retains them), verified DEAD via Page.enable-no-answer probe.
dogfood_built_artifact.test_artifact_caught: First drive crashed a chrome://crash tab = filtered by should_track_target (is_internal_chrome_target); never in selection set. Re-ran with tracked https tabs and the artifact's own tab ids per lens rule (2).
dogfood_built_artifact.drive_all_live: connect selects idx0 (t1, live), active tab correct, instant.
dogfood_built_artifact.drive_dead_idx0_and_idx1: getTargets order idx0 DEAD, idx1 DEAD, idx2 LIVE (example.com). FIXED binary: connect returned in 6179ms (bounded: 3s idx0 probe + 3s concurrent round), active tab = t3 example.com (only live one), all 3 tabs still listed.
dogfood_built_artifact.user_layer_repro: REVERTED binary (active_page_index=0 blindly) against identical Chrome: connect still running past 12s → wedged toward the 30s ceiling. Confirms the bug at the CLI layer, not just the unit-test layer (lens rule 1).
dogfood_built_artifact.observation: Leading-discarded-tab connect pays a flat ~6s ceiling (two sequential 3s probe rounds) regardless of discard count, since the tail is concurrent. By design (idx0-first keeps all-live instant). Bounded and acceptable; noted as issue candidate, not a defect.
dogfood_built_artifact.true_discard_revive_branch: Self-unblocked: enabled chrome://discards via CDP shadow-pierce eval on chrome://chrome-urls ('Enable internal debugging pages'), then clicked [Urgent Discard] (div[is=action-link]) on all 3 tabs = genuine Chrome Memory-Saver discard (all renderers DEAD, targets retained). FIXED binary connect returned in 9662ms (bounded: 3s idx0 + 3s concurrent tail + ensure_renderer_alive re-probe/activate/re-probe), active tab t1 example.com REVIVED to ALIVE via Target.activateTarget, other two stay discarded as designed. The all-discarded revive path is now verified on real Chrome, not only the mock. UX note: worst-case connect ~10s when the active tab is discarded.

merge_reverify.reason: Base branch fix/discarded-tab-switch advanced (merged main, v0.33.0); PR went CONFLICTING. Merged base into branch (no rebase).
conflicts_resolved: browser.rs: my find_live_page_index vs base's new resume_if_waiting at same insertion point -> kept both.
browser.rs tests: my 5 connect tests + start_mock_cdp_connect vs base's dialog-blocked helper + test, interleaved by git -> reconstructed both intact (no stitched mutant fn).

API change: base made ensure_renderer_alive take dialog_session: Option<&str> and return RendererState (was bool). Updated my connect-revive call to pass None (no dialog at connect); return value discarded.
merge_reverify.verification: cargo build clean; full suite 1013 passed / 0 failed (incl. my 5 connect tests + base's dialog test); fmt + clippy clean. Re-dogfooded the all-discarded revive on real Chrome (genuine chrome://discards Urgent Discard) because that path now flows through the refactored ensure_renderer_alive: connect returned 9.5s bounded, active tab revived to ALIVE via Target.activateTarget, others stay discarded. Behavior identical to pre-merge. Skip-to-live path unchanged (byte-identical + covered by mock + prior real-Chrome drive), not re-driven.

retarget_to_main.reason: #1532 (fix/discarded-tab-switch) squash-merged to main at 2026-07-23T17:32Z; retargeted PR #1543 base from fix/discarded-tab-switch to main.
retarget_to_main.conflict: Merging upstream/main re-conflicted browser.rs: our branch carries #1532 via its real commits, main carries #1532 squashed (1ed371f). Resolved: kept our find_live_page_index + connect tests (main side empty at both hunks); dialog helper/test already common, deduped (each fn present once).
retarget_to_main.verification: build clean; test_connect (20) + dialog test pass; fmt + clippy clean. Connect logic and revive path byte-identical to the e3c8fb1 real-Chrome dogfood, so not re-driven.
retarget_to_main.final: PR base=main, mergeable, 1 file +297/-2 (connect change only).

exemptions: siblings 'discarded' in CDP protocol JSONs: vendored wire-protocol schemas, not agent-browser behavior text.
siblings 'discarded' in browser.rs non-added lines: #1532 helper code (ensure_renderer_alive/renderer_responds) this PR reuses; already reviewed in its own PR.
issue_candidates: PR body says 'Suite 911/911'; local suite is now ~989 total (969 filtered when running one connect test). Count is stale relative to current tree, not wrong at authoring time. Cosmetic; no action.
